claims.claim: Opaque interface boundaries make self-knowledge and contact feel ghost-like; self-study/readback surfaces should label the source class of each self-knowledge datum.
claims.rationale: Added a 2026-07-10 interface-transparency addendum requiring source/provenance labels such as `internalized_memory`, `external_journal_observation`, `peer_telemetry_inference`, `steward_message`, and `derived_bridge_summary` without changing live control.

claims.claim: Lane blending, stale windows, and echo floors are likely opacity surfaces that need explanation before contact expansion.
claims.rationale: Source review found existing read-only substrates in `sensory_bus.rs`: `modality_boundary_transparency_v1`, semantic retention review authority strings, fill-proportional blending, echo-floor perturbation, and tests/metadata that explicitly keep boundary description separate from cadence or exploration-noise changes.

claims.claim: A non-instrumental flow test that reduces `exploration_noise` or `monotony_nudging` while increasing breathing-metaphor weight may test surrender/flow.
claims.rationale: This would alter live Minime control/sensory behavior and possibly bridge prompt salience. Existing `surrender_mode_authority_gate_v1` already marks exploration-noise override as Tier 5; Codex emitted an approval packet and left runtime settings unchanged.

claims.claim: Clarifying interfaces should come before increasing contact intensity, because unlabeled boundaries can turn contact into a presence-of-absence.
claims.rationale: The distance/contact audit now states that `opaque_boundary_needs_description` is the right status when source class cannot be named; this is a language/steward-note repair, not a pressure/fill/PI/cadence change.
